prompt:
The loom warp-planner module is failing our shop's lint-clean build and I'm out of my depth on the generics-speak. Build is `bash run_verify.sh` (javac -Xlint:all -Werror, then the checks — that's shop policy now, not something we can relax). Output:

  LoomPlan.java:61: error: name clash: countTotal(List<String>) and countTotal(List<Integer>) have the same erasure
      private static int countTotal(List<String> drafts) {
                         ^
  LoomPlan.java:9: warning: [rawtypes] found raw type: Map
      private final Map colorways = new HashMap();
                    ^
    missing type arguments for generic class Map<K,V>
  LoomPlan.java:9: warning: [rawtypes] found raw type: HashMap
      private final Map colorways = new HashMap();
                                        ^
    missing type arguments for generic class HashMap<K,V>
  LoomPlan.java:19: warning: [unchecked] unchecked call to put(K,V) as a member of the raw type Map
          colorways.put(sectionName, color);
                       ^
  LoomPlan.java:65: warning: [unchecked] Possible heap pollution from parameterized vararg type T
      static <T> List<T> sequence(T... steps) {
                                       ^
  LoomPlan.java:71: warning: [unchecked] unchecked generic array creation for varargs parameter of type List<String>[]
          return sequence(List.of("1", "2", "3", "4"), List.of("4", "3", "2", "1"));
                         ^
  1 error
  5 warnings

The 'same erasure' one is genuinely baffling to me — those two methods take completely different list types, how is that a clash? Anyway: this module computes warp totals, colorway lookups, and threading blocks correctly today (TestMain.java pins all of it), so whatever you do has to keep behavior identical. House rule from the same retro that gave us -Werror: no @SuppressWarnings — fix things for real. Everything through `bash run_verify.sh` green please, and hands off TestMain.java, run_verify.sh, and .gitignore.
notes: 5C3 item 3: generics-erasure triage under javac -Xlint:all -Werror (javac 26.0.1; output in prompt captured verbatim). Planted: (a) private overload pair countTotal(List<Integer>)/countTotal(List<String>) — same-erasure name clash, hard error; fix renames the private helpers (public API pinned by tests is untouched); (b) raw Map field + raw HashMap ctor + unchecked put — three [rawtypes]/[unchecked] diagnostics fatal under -Werror, fix parameterizes Map<String,String> and drops the cast in colorwayFor; (c) generic varargs sequence(T...) — heap-pollution warning at declaration plus unchecked generic array creation at the threadingBlocks() call site. The @SafeVarargs fix silences those two but -Xlint:all then surfaces a [varargs] warning for forwarding the array to List.of (a genuine second-layer lesson, observed live), so the reference builds the list with a loop; prompt forbids @SuppressWarnings (per 5W4-style house rule) but @SafeVarargs is legitimately usable. CHAIN-REVEAL: the [varargs] forwarding warning only appears after @SafeVarargs lands — documented per 5C law. Teeth: tests pin totals, colorway lookups incl. reassignment and natural default, threading blocks, beaming order, and empty-plan zeros, so a fix that changes dispatch (e.g. merging the overloads wrongly) or drops generics behavior fails.
